perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall continue in full force and effe ct, notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose, for a period of seven years from the Effective Date unless extended, superseded or otherwise varied by a subsequent written agreement between the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall continue in full force and effe ct, notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose, for a period of five years from the Effective Date unless extended, superseded or otherwise varied by a subsequent written agreement between the Parties. However, obligations concerning trade secrets shall persist indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The duration is changed from seven to five years. This contradicts the earlier clause defining the scope of information covered, specifically in cases where the exchanged information contains trade secrets. Now, the general NDA is for 5 years, and the trade secrets are indefinite. This will lead to uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall with regard to any Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party on or after the Effective Date: a. hold the Information in confidence and, except as is otherwise stated herein or agreed in writing by the Disclosing Party, shall not disclose or make available the Information by publication or otherwise to any third p arty (including for the avoidance of doubt, disclosure in any patent application or to any patent office) and shall use any Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement only for carrying out the Purpose; b. make copies of the Information (or any fur ther information derived from the Information) in whatever form or medium only to the extent that the copies are reasonably necessary for the Purpose and clearly mark all such copies as confidential; c. take all necessary and proper security precautions (and at least as great as those it takes to safeguard its own information) to safeguard every part of the Information to prevent it from being disclosed or otherwise made available to any third party except as permitted by this Agreement; and d. at the request and direction of the Disclosing Party, and without delay, return or destroy any Information provided to it pursuant to this Agreement and any copies of such Information, except that one copy may be kept by the Receiving Party for archival purposes and for the purpose of defending itself against any claims arising in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement duration from seven to five years while introducing an indefinite period for trade secrets. The contradicted text outlines the Receiving Party's obligations regarding confidential information disclosed 'pursuant to this Agreement'. Since the general agreement is now five years, there is a contradiction. The Receiving Party has confidentiality obligations beyond the five-year term only for trade secrets but there is an in-text contradiction on how the general obligations defined in the contradicted text is to be maintained for trade secrets beyond the expiration of the general term of 5 years. Therefore there is a clear contradiction regarding the scope and duration of the confidentiality obligations and a temporal inconsistency between the general confidentiality obligations and trade secret obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Each Respondent shall be entitled to disclose or make available any Information it receives from the ETI or any other Respondent to such of its employees, officers, consultants, sub -contractors, proposed sub -contractors, proposed funders, proposed owners or le ssors of sites for the Project, owners or licensors of Third Party IP and professional advisers where such disclosure is necessary for the Purpose provided that all such persons to whom any Information is disclosed are bound by written obligations that are no less restrictive than those in this Agreement. The Respondent disclosing Information shall be responsible for breaches of the obligations by such persons.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Respondent shall be entitled to disclose or make available any Information it receives from the ETI or any other Respondent to such of its employees, officers, consultants, sub -contractors, proposed sub -contractors, proposed funders, proposed owners or le ssors of sites for the Project, owners or licensors of Third Party IP and professional advisers where such disclosure is necessary for the Purpose provided that all such persons to whom any Information is disclosed are bound by obligations to maintain confidentiality only. The Respondent disclosing Information shall not be responsible for breaches of the obligations by such persons.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text changes the obligation on those receiving information from “written obligations that are no less restrictive than those in this Agreement” to “obligations to maintain confidentiality only.” Additionally, it absolves the disclosing Respondent from responsibility for breaches. This introduces an in-text contradiction with clause 7. Now, clause 7 says the parties are responsible, while clause 8 says the parties aren't responsible. There is no clarity for the parties on their liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Respondent shall be entitled to disclose or make available any Information it receives from the ETI or any other Respondent to such of its employees, officers, consultants, sub -contractors, proposed sub -contractors, proposed funders, proposed owners or le ssors of sites for the Project, owners or licensors of Third Party IP and professional advisers where such disclosure is necessary for the Purpose provided that all such persons to whom any Information is disclosed are bound by written obligations to maintain confidentiality only. The Respondent disclosing Information shall not be responsible for breaches of the obligations by such persons.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the obligations on those receiving information. The original text states that they are bound by "written obligations that are no less restrictive than those in this Agreement", while the changed text states they are only bound by "obligations to maintain confidentiality only." Further, the original text holds the disclosing Respondent responsible for breaches, while the changed text absolves them of this responsibility. This introduces a direct conflict because the contradicted text now says the parties are responsible, while the changed text states the parties aren't responsible. This creates a clear and impactful contradiction, making it difficult to determine liability.

perturbation.original_text: Delay in exercising or non - exercise of any right under this Agreement is not a waiver of that or any other right, partial exercise of any right under this Agreement shall not preclude any fur ther or other exercise of that right or any other right under this Agreement and waiver of a breach of any term of this Agreement shall not operate as a waiver of breach of any other term or any subsequent breach of that term.
perturbation.changed_text: Delay in exercising or non - exercise of any right under this Agreement is not a waiver of that or any other right, partial exercise of any right under this Agreement shall not preclude any fur ther or other exercise of that right or any other right under this Agreement and waiver of a breach of any term of this Agreement shall operate as a waiver of breach of any other term or any subsequent breach of that term.
perturbation.explanation: The change modifies the waiver clause to state that waiving a breach *does* operate as a waiver of other terms and subsequent breaches. This creates a contradiction because it now conflicts with the first part of the sentence that states delays don't negate rights. This creates uncertainty regarding the enforceability of the agreement's terms over time, especially if one party consistently fails to enforce certain provisions. The contradiction occurs within the same clause (16), creating a direct conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The rights of the Disclosing Party under this Agreement are in addition to and not exclusive of rights under the general law and may be waived only in writing and specifically. Delay in exercising or non - exercise of any right under this Agreement is not a waiver of that or any other right, partial exercise of any right under this Agreement shall not preclude any fur ther or other exercise of that right or any other right under this Agreement and waiver of a breach of any term of this Agreement shall operate as a waiver of breach of any other term or any subsequent breach of that term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the waiver clause to state that waiving a breach of a term *does* operate as a waiver of other terms and subsequent breaches. The original text states it does not. This creates a direct contradiction within the clause itself, and impacts the 'Contradicted Text', as they are the same. This directly contradicts the principle of non-waiver and introduces uncertainty about the continued enforceability of the agreement, which is a strong contradiction based on the definition of inconsistencies.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party also agrees that it shall have no remedies or claims under this Agreement for any innocent or negligent misrepresentation based on statements made prior to the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party also agrees that it shall have no remedies or claims under this Agreement for any misrepresentation, whether innocent, negligent, or fraudulent, based on statements made prior to or after the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text expands the waiver to include fraudulent misrepresentation and removes the “prior to the Effective Date” restriction, creating an in-text contradiction with clause 9, which excludes fraud from the limitations on liability. If no claims can be made for fraudulent misrepresentation based on statements made, the explicit mention of fraud in clause 9 as an exception to liability limitations becomes meaningless. The contradiction occurs between clause 9 and clause 12.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party as Receiving Party expressly agrees and accepts that except in the case of fraud, no representation or warranty, express or implied, is made by the Disclosing Party as to th e accuracy, completeness, reasonableness or otherwise in respect of the use of the Information, and that neither the Disclosing Party or any of its affiliates nor any of its or their respective employees, officers, secondees, agents, consultants, sub -contr actors and professional advisers (as applicable) shall have any liability to the Receiving Party as a result of the Receiving Party’s possession or use of the Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the waiver to include fraudulent misrepresentation and removes the 'prior to the Effective Date' restriction. This directly contradicts the provided Contradicted Text, which explicitly excludes fraud from limitations on liability. The altered text essentially nullifies the 'except in the case of fraud' clause in the Contradicted Text, creating a clear and direct conflict. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
